claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start by asking:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you'd like to inquire about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child - my son Alex - and I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for your son Alex.

Let me start with some questions about Alex:

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Alex is 13, so he's under 16 which is relevant for Child Benefit eligibility.

Now I need to ask about your relationship with Alex and your living situation:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to understand a bit more about your immigration and residency status:

**Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another immigration status that gives you the right to reside here?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen, so I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask about whether anyone else might be claiming Child Benefit for Alex:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask about Alex's education and work status:

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?** (This would include things like being in school, college doing A-levels, or approved vocational training - but not university degree courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's 13, so he's still in secondary school - just regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, that makes sense. Since Alex is 13 and in regular secondary school, that's considered full-time education.

Let me ask one more important question:

**Is Alex currently in local authority care, or has he been in hospital or residential care for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in care or hospital - he lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just check one final thing:

**Are you responsible for Alex? That is, do you provide for him financially and make decisions about his upbringing?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm his parent and I provide for him and make all the decisions about his upbringing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, I can now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review what you've told me:

- Alex is 13 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you
- You live in the UK and are a UK citizen (have the right to reside)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is in full-time education (secondary school)
- Alex is not in local authority care or long-term hospital care
- You are responsible for Alex as his parent

**Good news! Based on the eligibility criteria, you appear to be eligible to claim Child Benefit for your son Alex.**

You meet all the key requirements:
- You're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education or training)
- The child lives with you
- You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
- No one else is claiming for the same child

**Next steps:** You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. You'll need Alex's birth certificate when you apply.

**Important note about the High Income Child Benefit Charge:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 a year, you may have to pay a tax charge. If either of you earns £80,000 or more, the charge equals the amount of Child Benefit received. You can still claim and choose not to receive the payments if you wish, which can protect your National Insurance credits.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's brilliant, thank you so much for your help! You've explained everything really clearly. I understand what I need to do now and I'll get the application sorted out. Thanks again!
